contentBlocks.original:
भास्वान् काश्यपगोत्रजोऽरुणरुचिर्यः सिंहपोऽर्कः समि-
-त्षट्त्रिस्थोऽदशशोभनो गुरुशशी भौमाः सुमित्राः सदा ।
शुक्रो मन्दरिपुः कलिङ्गजनपश्चाग्नीश्वरौ देवते
मध्येवर्तुलपूर्वदिग्दिनकरः कुर्यात्सदा मङ्गलम् ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhāsvān kāśyapagōtrajō'ruṇaruchiryaḥ siṃhapō'rkaḥ sami-
-tṣaṭtristhō'daśaśōbhanō guruśaśī bhaumāḥ sumitrāḥ sadā ।
śukrō mandaripuḥ kaḻiṅgajanapaśchāgnīśvarau dēvatē
madhyēvartulapūrvadigdinakaraḥ kuryātsadā maṅgaḻam ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sūrya (Sun) — of the Kaśyapa gotra, with tawny-red radiance, ruler of Leo, having a planet as his special oblation-fire — in the sixth and third positions is beneficial; the moon, Jupiter, and Mars are friends. Salutations to Sūrya, who gives great results to those who bow to him.
contentBlocks.meanings: bhāsvān: Sūrya (Sun), the radiant one | kāśyapa-gotra-ja: of the Kaśyapa lineage | aruṇa-rucir: tawny-red radiance | siṃha-po: ruler of Leo | arkaḥ: the Sun | ṣaṭ-tri-stha: beneficial in the sixth and third positions | daśa-śobhana: giving great results | guru-śaśī-bhaumāḥ: Jupiter, Moon, and Mars | su-mitrāḥ: good friends

contentBlocks.original:
चन्द्रः कर्कटकप्रभुः सितनिभश्चात्रेयगोत्रोद्भव-
-श्चात्रेयश्चतुरश्रवारुणमुखश्चापे उमाधीश्वरः ।
षट्सप्ताग्नि दशैकशोभनफलो नोरिर्बुधार्कौप्रियौ
स्वामी यामुनजश्च पर्णसमिधः कुर्यात्सदा मङ्गलम् ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chandraḥ karkaṭakaprabhuḥ sitanibhaśchātrēyagōtrōdbhava-
-śchātrēyaśchaturaśravāruṇamukhaśchāpē umādhīśvaraḥ ।
ṣaṭsaptāgni daśaikaśōbhanaphalō nōrirbudhārkaupriyau
svāmī yāmunajaścha parṇasamidhaḥ kuryātsadā maṅgaḻam ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Candra (Moon) — lord of Cancer, of white complexion, born of the Atreya gotra, himself also an Atreya — square (Vāruṇa aspect), at the highest position in Libra — O Uma's lord (Śiva), who is favorable through him. Salutations to Candra who illumines the lotus-like minds of devotees.
contentBlocks.meanings: candraḥ: Moon | karkaṭaka-prabhuḥ: lord of Cancer | sita-nibha: white complexion | atreya-gotra-udbhava: born of the Atreya lineage | catur-aśra: square/quadrant (astrological aspect) | vāruṇa-mukha: facing Varuṇa (western aspect) | uca-sthāna: exalted position (Taurus) | umā-adhīśvara: lord of Umā (Śiva, favorable through the moon)

contentBlocks.original:
भौमो दक्षिणदिक्त्रिकोणयमदिग्विन्ध्येश्वरः खादिरः
स्वामी वृश्चिकमेषयोस्तु सुगुरुश्चार्कः शशी सौहृदः ।
ज्ञोऽरिः षट्त्रिफलप्रदश्च वसुधास्कन्दौ क्रमाद्देवते
भारद्वाजकुलोद्वहोऽरुणरुचिः कुर्यात्सदा मङ्गलम् ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhaumō dakṣiṇadiktrikōṇayamadigvindhyēśvaraḥ khādiraḥ
svāmī vṛśchikamēṣayōstu suguruśchārkaḥ śaśī sauhṛdaḥ ।
jñō'riḥ ṣaṭtriphalapradaścha vasudhāskandau kramāddēvatē
bhāradvājakulōdvahō'ruṇaruchiḥ kuryātsadā maṅgaḻam ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Bhūma/Maṅgala (Mars) — lord of the southern direction, of the triangle of Yama's direction, Vindhyeśvara — lord of Vṛścika (Scorpio) and Meṣa (Aries) — with the Sun and Moon as good friends — the red planet — salutations to Mars who grants strength.
contentBlocks.meanings: bhaumaḥ: Mars (son of the earth, Bhūmi-putra) | dakṣiṇa-dik: southern direction | tri-koṇa: triangular/trine (astrological aspect) | yama-dig: Yama's direction (south) | vindhyeśvara: lord associated with the Vindhya mountain | vṛścika-meṣa-sva-guru: lord of Scorpio and Aries | arka-śaśī-sauhṛda: friendship with Sun and Moon | rakta: red

contentBlocks.original:
सौम्यः पीत उदङ्मुखः समिदपामार्गोऽत्रिगोत्रोद्भवो
बाणेशानदिशः सुहृद्रविसुतः शान्तः सुतः शीतगोः ।
कन्यायुग्मपतिर्दशाष्टचतुरः षण्णेत्रगः शोभनो
विष्णुर्देव्यधिदेवते मगधपः कुर्यात्सदा मङ्गलम् ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
saumyaḥ pīta udaṅmukhaḥ samidapāmārgō'trigōtrōdbhavō
bāṇēśānadiśaḥ suhṛdravisutaḥ śāntaḥ sutaḥ śītagōḥ ।
kanyāyugmapatirdaśāṣṭachaturaḥ ṣaṇṇētragaḥ śōbhanō
viṣṇurdēvyadhidēvatē magadhapaḥ kuryātsadā maṅgaḻam ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Budha (Mercury) — yellow-hued, facing north, with apāmārga as his oblation-plant, of the Atri gotra — associated with the northeastern direction — peaceable, son of the Moon, son of the cold-rayed one (Moon) — salutations to Mercury the auspicious.
contentBlocks.meanings: saumyaḥ: Mercury (literally: of Soma/Moon, auspicious) | pīta: yellow | udaṅ-mukhaḥ: facing north | apāmārga-samit: apāmārga plant as oblation | atri-gotra-udbhava: of the Atri gotra | bāṇa-īśāna-diśaḥ: northeastern direction | suhṛt-ravi-sutaḥ: friendly with son of Ravi (Sun's son — Saturn?) | śāntaḥ: peaceable | śīta-goḥ sutaḥ: son of the cold-rayed one (Moon)

contentBlocks.original:
जीवश्चाङ्गिरगोत्रजोत्तरमुखो दीर्घोत्तराशास्थितः
पीतोऽश्वत्थसमिच्च सिन्धुजनितश्चापोऽथ मीनाधिपः ।
सूर्येन्दुक्षितिजाः प्रिया बुधसितौ शत्रू समाश्चापरे
सप्तद्वे नवपञ्चमे शुभकरः कुर्यात्सदा मङ्गलम् ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jīvaśchāṅgiragōtrajōttaramukhō dīrghōttarāśāsthitaḥ
pītō'śvatthasamichcha sindhujanitaśchāpō'tha mīnādhipaḥ ।
sūryēndukṣitijāḥ priyā budhasitau śatrū samāśchāparē
saptadvē navapañchamē śubhakaraḥ kuryātsadā maṅgaḻam ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Guru (Jupiter) — of the Āṅgira gotra, facing north, long-bodied in the northern direction — yellow-complexioned, with pippala (fig tree) as his sacred oblation — born from the ocean of Sindhu — lord of Pisces — salutations to Jupiter, the most auspicious.
contentBlocks.meanings: jīvaḥ: Jupiter (Guru, life-giver) | āṅgira-gotra-ja: of the Āṅgira lineage (Bṛhaspati is son of Aṅgiras) | uttara-mukha: facing north | dīrgha-uttarā-sthita: long-bodied in the northern direction | pīta: yellow | aśvattha-samit: pippala (sacred fig, Ficus religiosa) as oblation | sindhu-janita: born from the Sindhu ocean | cāpa-mīna-adhipa: lord of Pisces (and Sagittarius)

contentBlocks.original:
शुक्रो भार्गवगोत्रजः सितरुचिः पूर्वामुखः पूर्वदिक्
पाञ्चालस्थ वृषस्तुलाधिपमहाराष्ट्राधिपौदुम्बरः ।
इन्द्राणीमघवा बुधश्च रविजो मित्रोर्क चन्द्रावरी
षष्ठत्रिर्दशवर्जिते भृगुसुतः कुर्यात्सदा मङ्गलम् ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śukrō bhārgavagōtrajaḥ sitaruchiḥ pūrvāmukhaḥ pūrvadik
pāñchālastha vṛṣastulādhipamahārāṣṭrādhipaudumbaraḥ ।
indrāṇīmaghavā budhaścha ravijō mitrōrka chandrāvarī
ṣaṣṭhatrirdaśavarjitē bhṛgusutaḥ kuryātsadā maṅgaḻam ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Śukra (Venus) — of the Bhārgava gotra, white in radiance, facing east, eastern direction — the great ruler of the Pāñcāla region, lord of Taurus and Libra, lord of Mahārāṣṭra — with the udumbara tree [as his sacred plant] — salutations to the bright-rayed Venus.
contentBlocks.meanings: śukraḥ: Venus (the bright/pure one) | bhārgava-gotra-ja: of the Bhṛgu lineage (Śukra is son of Bhṛgu) | sita-ruciḥ: white radiance | pūrva-mukhaḥ: facing east | pūrva-dik: eastern direction | pāñcāla-stha: ruler of the Pāñcāla region | vṛṣa-tulā-adhipa: lord of Taurus and Libra | mahārāṣṭra-adhipa: lord of Mahārāṣṭra | audumbara: udumbara tree (Ficus racemosa)

contentBlocks.original:
मन्दः कृष्णनिभः सपश्चिममुखः सौराष्ट्रपः काश्यपः
स्वामी नक्रसुकुम्भयोर्बुधसितौ मित्रौ कुजेन्दू द्विषौ ।
स्थानं पश्चिमदिक् प्रजापतियमौ देवौ धनुर्धारकः
षट्त्रिस्थः शुभकृच्छनी रविसुतः कुर्यात्सदा मङ्गलम् ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mandaḥ kṛṣṇanibhaḥ sapaśchimamukhaḥ saurāṣṭrapaḥ kāśyapaḥ
svāmī nakrasukumbhayōrbudhasitau mitrau kujēndū dviṣau ।
sthānaṃ paśchimadik prajāpatiyamau dēvau dhanurdhārakaḥ
ṣaṭtristhaḥ śubhakṛchChanī ravisutaḥ kuryātsadā maṅgaḻam ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Śani (Saturn) — dark in complexion, facing west, with Saurāṣṭra as his region, of the Kaśyapa gotra — lord of Capricorn and Aquarius — Budha and Śukra are his friends — Mars and the Moon are enemies — salutations to Saturn who protects devotees.
contentBlocks.meanings: mandaḥ: Saturn (the slow-moving one) | kṛṣṇa-nibhaḥ: dark complexion | sa-paścima-mukhaḥ: facing west | saurāṣṭra-paḥ: ruler of Saurāṣṭra (Gujarat) | kāśyapaḥ: of the Kaśyapa gotra | nakra-su-kumbha: lord of Capricorn and Aquarius | budha-sita: Mercury and Venus as friends | kujendu: Mars and Moon as enemies

contentBlocks.original:
राहुः सिंहलदेशपोऽपि सतमः कृष्णाङ्गशूर्पासनो
यः पैठीनसगोत्रसम्भवसमिद्दूर्वामुखो दक्षिणः ।
यः सर्पः पशुदैवतोऽखिलगतः सूर्यग्रहे छादकः
षट्त्रिस्थः शुभकृच्च सिंहकसुतः कुर्यात्सदा मङ्गलम् ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rāhuḥ siṃhaḻadēśapō'pi satamaḥ kṛṣṇāṅgaśūrpāsanō
yaḥ paiṭhīnasagōtrasambhavasamiddūrvāmukhō dakṣiṇaḥ ।
yaḥ sarpaḥ paśudaivatō'khilagataḥ sūryagrahē Chādakaḥ
ṣaṭtristhaḥ śubhakṛchcha siṃhakasutaḥ kuryātsadā maṅgaḻam ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Rāhu — lord of Siṃhala (Sri Lanka) — though shadowy, dark-bodied, seated on a winnowing basket — of the Paitīnasa gotra — with dūrvā grass as his oblation, facing south — he who is served by Śarpa-s (serpents) — who in the seventh house is favorable for [some] — salutations to Rāhu.
contentBlocks.meanings: rāhuḥ: Rāhu (ascending lunar node) | siṃhala-deśa-pa: lord of the Siṃhala (Sri Lanka) region | sa-tamaḥ: though shadowy/dark | kṛṣṇa-aṅga: dark-bodied | śūrpa-āsana: seated on a winnowing basket | paitīnasa-gotra-sambhava: of the Paitīnasa gotra | dūrvā-mukha: with dūrvā grass | dakṣiṇaḥ: facing south | śarpa: serpents

contentBlocks.original:
केतुर्जैमिनिगोत्रजः कुशसमिद्वायव्यकोणेस्थित-
-श्चित्राङ्कध्वजलाञ्छनो हि भगवान् यो दक्षिणाशामुखः ।
ब्रह्मा चैव तु चित्रगुप्तपतिमान् प्रीत्याधिदेवः सदा
षट्त्रिस्थः शुभकृच्च बर्बरपतिः कुर्यात्सदा मङ्गलम् ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kēturjaiminigōtrajaḥ kuśasamidvāyavyakōṇēsthita-
-śchitrāṅkadhvajalāñChanō hi bhagavān yō dakṣiṇāśāmukhaḥ ।
brahmā chaiva tu chitraguptapatimān prītyādhidēvaḥ sadā
ṣaṭtristhaḥ śubhakṛchcha barbarapatiḥ kuryātsadā maṅgaḻam ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Ketu — of the Jaimini gotra, with kuśa grass as oblation, situated in the vāyavya (northwestern) corner — with a flag marked by a spotted/speckled emblem — the divine lord who faces south — who gives wealth and prosperity when propitiated — salutations to Ketu.
contentBlocks.meanings: ketuḥ: Ketu (descending lunar node) | jaimini-gotra-ja: of the Jaimini gotra | kuśa-samit: kuśa grass as sacred oblation | vāyavya-koṇe-sthita: situated in the northwestern corner | citra-aṅka-dhvaja-lāñchana: flag marked by a spotted emblem | dakṣiṇāśā-mukha: facing south | bhagavān: the divine lord
iti: thus ends | nava-graha maṅgala stotram: the auspicious hymn to the nine planets (Navagrahas)
